headline: 02 June 2026
subtitle: No new Kuwait attack on June 2 confirmed; June 1 diplomatic fallout continues; MOU unsigned; Hormuz closed

metrics.today: NR (no new confirmed attack in coverage window)

metrics.metric: Days since last confirmed cyber incident (Gulf FS)

items.bold_heading: No new civil defense alerts or air raid sirens reported in Kuwait on 2 June 2026
items.text: No KUNA, Kuwait Army, or Kuwait MOI advisory for a new or separate attack on 2 June confirmed at time of compilation. June 1 attack diplomatic fallout continued into June 2 news cycle, but these are follow-on statements, not new incident notifications.

items.bold_heading: Elevated civil defense posture from June 1 carries forward — no all-clear formally issued
items.text: Kuwait MOI has not issued a formal downgrade of the heightened readiness posture. Citizens and residents should continue to monitor KUNA and MOI channels.

items.bold_heading: No confirmed new strikes on Kuwait during coverage window (1 June 07:00 – 2 June 07:00 UTC+3)
items.text: The IRGC missile and drone attack confirmed in Report #83 occurred on the morning of 1 June, prior to this window's open. No Tier 1 primary source has confirmed a second distinct attack during this window. Diplomatic condemnations from GCC, Arab League, Muslim World League, and Jordan continued through June 2.

items.bold_heading: Kuwait foreign ministry condemns June 1 attack; invokes UN SC Resolution 2817 (2026)
items.text: Kuwait describes June 1 strikes as 'dangerous escalation and direct assault on security and stability,' violation of international law and UN Charter. Kuwait's UN envoy in Geneva called on Security Council to address Iranian violations, linking Hormuz blockade and attacks to global energy security risk.

items.bold_heading: Strait of Hormuz: ~4 vessels transiting (live); effective closure sustained
items.text: IRGC blockade and unresolved mine-clearance unchanged. MOU mine-clearance trigger (30 days post-signature) not yet activated.

items.bold_heading: KWI Terminal 1 phased reopening — Day 2; no new disruption confirmed
items.text: T1 gradual resumption continues. Kuwait Airways, Jazeera, Emirates, Saudia, Qatar Airways, Etihad, flydubai confirmed for T1. Oman Air and SalamAir mid-June. Monitor individual airline schedules before booking.

items.bold_heading: Banking and public sector — second normal working day post-Eid
items.text: Banks and government entities resumed 1 June; June 2 is a normal working day. Staff commute guidance reflects elevated security posture carry-forward from June 1.

items.bold_heading: Shuwaikh Port — ISPS Level 2, daylight-only operations carry forward
items.text: No confirmed advisory change from prior 05:00–19:00 UTC+3 operational window.

items.bold_heading: Land borders — carry-forward
items.text: Al-Nuwaisib (Saudi) post-Eid normalization continuing. Abdali (Iraq/Safwan) maintains elevated short-notice closure risk.

items.bold_heading: Civil defense remote-work directive in place
items.text: Physical attendance capped at 30% per Civil Service Commission; remainder working remotely.

MOU still unsigned on Day 95. US-Iran negotiators reached tentative 60-day extension by 28 May; Trump yet to sign. VP Vance: 'going back and forth on a couple of language points.' Iran Foreign Ministry cites lack of trust, shifting US positions, and Israel Lebanon operations as delay factors.

US-IRGC retaliatory cycle unresolved. US CENTCOM self-defence strikes (30–31 May, Goruk and Qeshm Island) → IRGC retaliation on Kuwait 1 June. CENTCOM signalled readiness to resume full combat. Neither side has declared ceasefire formally terminated.
Strait of Hormuz structurally closed; MOU mine-clearance trigger not activated. Live data: ~4 transits/day.

items.bold_heading: Sustained elevated security posture — no all-clear; maintain BCP readiness
items.text: No new Kuwait attack on June 2 confirmed, but heightened readiness posture from June 1 not formally lifted. Operate normally while keeping emergency response contacts active.

items.bold_heading: Oil: Brent $94.58, WTI ~$92 — ongoing review of energy-linked positions
items.text: Prices jumped +4% on June 1 month-open following IRGC strike and are partially retracing. Unsigned MOU and sustained structural Hormuz closure maintain supply-side pressure. Review collateral and margin thresholds.

items.bold_heading: Compliance: clear Eid SAR/OFAC backlog by EOD June 2
items.text: 26–31 May Eid gap in SAR filings under FinCEN FIN-2026-Alert002 (IRGC shadow fleet transactions) and OFAC screening queues should be fully resolved today — second working day post-holiday.

items.bold_heading: Cyber retaliation window remains elevated through at least 3–4 June
items.text: June 1 IRGC kinetic exchange sustains the 48–72h hacktivist and APT retaliation cycle. Active threat groups: 313 Team, ALTOUFAN TEAM, DieNet, Fatimion. Maintain elevated WAF, DDoS, and SIEM thresholds.

items.bold_heading: KWI T1 Day 2: monitor airline schedules before business travel
items.text: Not all carriers have confirmed individual flight resumption timings. Verify flight status before proceeding to airport.

items.bold_heading: JWC JWLA-033 listed area unchanged — June 1 attack the latest confirmation of no GCC sanctuary
items.text: War Risk Premiums remain 3–8% of hull value per voyage. No WRP reduction basis until MOU signed and mine-clearance commences.

items.bold_heading: Hormuz ~4 vessels live; IRGC permit framework unchanged — no transit normalisation basis
items.text: IRGC June 1 28-transit claim unverified. DFC government insurance backstop applies only to US-sponsored transits; independently-routed commercial transit coverage unresolved.

items.bold_heading: P&I Club policy review outstanding for IRGC-permit-dependent transits
items.text: IRGC Persian Gulf Strait Authority permit requirement creates novel exclusion clause exposure (permit denial; hostile action during transit). Obtain explicit written P&I/H&M confirmation before authorising Gulf shipments.

articles.title: The Unintended Consequences of Iran's Asymmetric Strategy and America's AI War
articles.author: Carnegie Endowment for International Peace (staff analysis)
articles.summary: Examines how Iran's asymmetric tools (proxies, missiles, drones) fragment the battlefield and prolong hostilities, and how AI-enabled US targeting — with compressed kill chains and largely symbolic human oversight — risks eroding international humanitarian law's restraint principles. The combination generates persistent retaliatory cycles and legitimacy costs that may outweigh tactical gains.
articles.relevance: The asymmetric + AI-enabled war model produces a multi-year threat environment — BCP, risk appetite, and insurance assumptions require structural rather than episodic calibration.

items.bold_heading: Elevated hacktivist DDoS/defacement threat — 48–72h window from June 1 extends through 3–4 June
items.text: 313 Team, ALTOUFAN TEAM, DieNet historically launch campaigns within 48–72h of IRGC kinetic exchanges. Financial services, e-payment platforms, and government portals in Kuwait and Gulf GCC are priority targets.
items.recommended_action: Maintain WAF sensitivity at elevated setting, keep DDoS mitigation on standby, confirm ISP/CDN DDoS response contacts are reachable.

items.bold_heading: IRGC-aligned APT credential phishing — Seedworm and TA453 active against Gulf FS
items.text: Seedworm confirmed on financial sector networks since February 2026 including US bank breach. TA453 active in credential phishing. Plausible lure themes: KWI reopening notifications, civil defense updates, MOU announcement spoofs.
items.recommended_action: Issue staff awareness notice on phishing lures themed around conflict developments; enforce MFA on all remote access.

items.bold_heading: No new CVE-specific advisory in past 24–48h for Gulf FS infrastructure
items.text: CISA April 7, 2026 advisory (AA26-097a) on Iranian-affiliated APT targeting critical infrastructure carries forward.
items.recommended_action: Maintain existing patch cycle; verify OT/SCADA patch compliance per CISA AA26-097a.

items.bold_heading: IRGC APT dual-track posture (espionage + destructive pre-positioning) — HIGH
items.text: Seedworm/MuddyWater and APT42 continuous against Gulf financial and government targets since February 2026. Priority: SWIFT monitoring for sanctions-evasion intel; data exfiltration; OT-adjacent pre-positioning.

items.bold_heading: Iranian ransomware-masquerading tactics for destructive attacks — MODERATE
items.text: Iranian APTs deploying intrusions masquerading as ransomware (Chaos variant) for destructive outcomes while maintaining deniability. Complicates incident attribution and insurance claim processing.

items.bold_heading: Iranian cyber capability evolution: AI-assisted recon, Gulf infrastructure expansion — MODERATE
items.text: Trellix 2026 report documents expansion into AI-assisted recon and lateral movement; energy, water, financial sectors in Gulf identified as priority targets.
items.assessed_likelihood: Moderate (escalation to High if MOU fails)

items.text: MOU signing decision (Trump) — announcement or breakdown signal
items.why_it_matters: If signed, 60-day clock for Hormuz mine-clearance and WRP reassessment begins; if talks collapse, kinetic escalation and oil price spike risk materialises.

items.text: KWI T1 phased reopening — carrier schedule confirmations, Day 2 and beyond
items.why_it_matters: Not all carriers confirmed; Qatar Airways (tentative 16 June), Oman Air, SalamAir timelines pending. Affects staff business travel planning.

items.text: Any new KUNA or Kuwait Army advisory — would signal a fresh incident post-coverage-window
items.why_it_matters: In the absence of such an advisory, June 1 remains the last confirmed kinetic event in Kuwait.

source_confidence.note: No new alert on June 2 confirmed by absence of KUNA/MOI advisory; consistent with ground-level observation
Arab Times and The National (Tier 1) confirm attack was June 1; no June 2 incident in primary sources
KWI Day 2 status based on June 1 opening reports; carry-forwards clearly labelled
Multiple Tier 1 sources corroborate MOU status and US-IRGC retaliatory cycle
Derived from confirmed June 1 incident and oil price data from TradingEconomics
JWC listing and WRP range confirmed; P&I advisory extended-window carry-forward
No new CVE advisory; hacktivist threat level based on established post-kinetic pattern
Capability assessments from commercial intel; no new June 2-specific strategic signal

footer: Report #84 compiled 2026-06-02 at 07:30 UTC+3. CORRECTED VERSION (v2) — v1 erroneously reported June 2 attacks based on unverified secondary sources (alexa.ng, nomadlawyer.org); corrected per Tier 1 source verification and ground-level confirmation.
